pr.title: Match a function for Fox and Ness thanks to altwo
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched two fighter attribute-copy routines for Fox and Ness by replacing hand-written/standalone assembly with C struct assignment copies. Fox's func_800E576C was removed from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s and implemented in src/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.c, while Ness's inline asm func_80114B2C in src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.c was converted to C. The PR also added or refined ftFoxAttributes and ftNessAttributes struct layouts enough for the compiler to emit the same bulk-copy behavior.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementation of func_800E576C from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s.

Added a C implementation of func_800E576C(Fighter* ft) in ftfox.c that copies ft->x10C_ftData->ext_attr into ft->x2D8_specialAttributes2 and then assigns ft->x2D4_specialAttributes to that copied attribute block.
Added ftFoxAttributes to ftfox.h with explicit f32 fields at offsets x0, x4, x8, xC and filler through 0xD1, enabling struct assignment to reproduce the original copy loop.
Replaced Ness inline asm func_80114B2C(HSD_GObj* gobj) with C that gets Fighter* from gobj->user_data and copies ft->x10C_ftData->ext_attr into ft->x2D4_specialAttributes.
Refined ftNessAttributes in ftness.h from a mostly opaque 0x94-byte blob plus unk94 into a larger layout with named initial f32 fields, unk94, and trailing filler through 0xD9.
Removed local pragma push/pop/peephole scaffolding around the former Ness inline asm.
smart_moves: Recognized the assembly loops as fixed-size struct copies from fighter data ext_attr into per-instance special attributes, allowing the code to collapse to simple C assignment: *sA2 = *ext_attr.
Used typed fighter-specific attribute structs to make the compiler generate bulk-copy code instead of manually reproducing the loop.
For Fox, preserved the semantic difference that the destination is x2D8_specialAttributes2 but x2D4_specialAttributes is then pointed at that same copied block.
For Ness, preserved the existing destination as x2D4_specialAttributes and correctly obtained the Fighter from HSD_GObj user_data before copying.
Adjusted struct sizes/fillers rather than naming unknown fields prematurely, which is appropriate when only offsets and copy size are evidenced.
decomp_lessons: When PowerPC assembly is a counted loop loading/storing paired words from ext_attr into a fighter special-attribute buffer, try modeling it as a whole-struct assignment between fighter-specific attribute structs.

Matching a struct-copy routine depends on the destination and source pointer fields as much as the loop body; Fox uses x2D8_specialAttributes2 as the copy target and then updates x2D4_specialAttributes, while Ness copies directly into x2D4_specialAttributes.
Opaque filler arrays are useful for forcing the known struct size needed by compiler-generated copy loops while leaving unknown attributes unnamed.
Header struct layout changes can be the key to eliminating assembly: the C code is short, but the struct's total size and alignment drive whether the emitted copy matches.
Avoid inventing semantic names for unknown attribute fields; this PR kept generic names such as x0, x4, x8, xC, unk94, data, and data2.
naming_conventions: Fighter-specific attribute structs use the pattern ftFoxAttributes and ftNessAttributes.
Unknown fields are named by offset when scalar, such as x0, x4, x8, xC, and unk94.
Unknown filler arrays use generic names such as data, data2, or data_filler_10 with size expressions based on known offsets.
Matched functions retained their existing placeholder symbol names: func_800E576C and func_80114B2C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The removed Fox assembly used ctr = 0x1A plus a trailing word copy, effectively copying the ftFoxAttributes block from ext_attr to x2D8_specialAttributes2 before storing that pointer into x2D4_specialAttributes.
The removed Ness inline asm used ctr = 0x1B plus a trailing word copy, copying from ft->x10C_ftData->ext_attr into ft->x2D4_specialAttributes.
C struct assignment was sufficient to replace repeated lwzu/stwu word-pair copy loops.
Struct filler lengths were tuned to match the expected copied byte count and thereby reproduce the original loop shape.
Removing inline asm also allowed removal of surrounding pragma peephole scaffolding in ftness.c.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter OnLoad or attribute-copy routines that copy x10C_ftData->ext_attr into x2D4_specialAttributes or x2D8_specialAttributes2.
Search for remaining PowerPC counted copy loops using mtctr with lwzu/stwu pairs in fighter character asm files.
Compare ftFoxAttributes and ftNessAttributes sizes against later recovered attribute structs to replace filler fields with real names when evidence exists.
Look for other PRs crediting altwo for struct-copy matching tactics.
